name: tech-edge-ai-overview
description: Use web_search to search for 'edge AI inference frameworks on-device deployment 2025 2026'. Summarize the edge AI landscape in 3 bullet points. Keep response under 100 words.

ground_truth.reference_answer:
- The edge AI inference landscape in 2025-2026 is driven by frameworks like LiteRT (formerly TFLite), llama.cpp, ONNX Runtime, ExecuTorch, and Core ML, enabling on-device deployment of sub-billion to 3B parameter models on smartphones, PCs, and IoT devices.
- Key drivers are latency reduction (eliminating cloud round-trips), privacy (data never leaves the device), cost savings (offloading inference to user hardware), and offline availability.
- Compact models such as Llama 3.2 (1B/3B), Gemma 3 (270M+), Phi-4 mini (3.8B), and Qwen2.5 (0.5B-1.5B) now handle practical tasks on-device, aided by INT4/INT8 quantization and NPUs exceeding 45 TOPS in flagship chips.
key_facts: LiteRT (formerly TFLite) delivers 1.4x faster GPU performance than TFLite and adds NPU acceleration
Sub-billion parameter models now handle many practical on-device tasks thanks to distillation and high-quality synthetic training data
Four key drivers of edge AI adoption are latency, privacy, cost, and offline availability
Major compact models for edge include Llama 3.2, Gemma 3, Phi-4 mini, SmolLM2, and Qwen2.5
Hybrid edge-cloud for agentic AI can yield energy savings up to 75% and cost reductions over 80%
